mechanism_narrative: Elevated industrial production levels reflect expanding manufacturing and utilities output that support corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 6.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 6.0% and S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a near-zero federal funds rate creates negative real interest rates that force investors out of cash and into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a pooled regression, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only when the unemployment rate is elevated above 5.5%, because consumption resilience in slack labor markets indicates demand durability; the signal is null when labor markets are tight.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5%, if month-over-month RSAFS increases do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of cases over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% signals an exceptionally accommodative monetary policy stance that suppresses short-term discount rates and stimulates risk-taking, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of such months over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 104 indicates a contraction in manufacturing and utilities output that precedes downward earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If INDPRO < 104 and 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered quarters over any 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals broad-based demand expansion across labor and construction sectors, amplifying cyclical equity exposure and generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If both proxies meet their thresholds but 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of joint trigger events over a 2-year window, or if either proxy alone produces equal or stronger predictive power, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls falling below 138000 indicates a contracting labor market that reduces aggregate income and consumer spending forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS < 138000 and 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over any 3-year window, reject.
